steps.title: Use the measurement problem
steps.content: The study aims to evaluate conservation strategies for white-headed langurs. But the passage says accurately counting these primates is challenging, making it hard to know whether the population is rising, falling, or stable.

steps.title: Infer the research risk
steps.content: Choice B invents a new counting method. Choice C adds other species without support. Choice D says the team ignores approaches, which the passage never suggests. If population change cannot be measured reliably, strategy effectiveness may be judged inaccurately. The answer is A.
